title: Zustand stores for transactions, categories, people
description: Create stores/transactionStore.ts, stores/categoryStore.ts, stores/peopleStore.ts. Each store loads from SQLite on init and keeps in-memory state in sync after mutations. Expose typed actions: add, update, remove, setMonth.

title: Repository layer — transactions, categories, people
description: Implement db/repositories/transactions.ts (insert, update, delete, listByMonth, getById), db/repositories/categories.ts (list, insert, update, delete), db/repositories/people.ts (list, insert, update, delete). All queries typed with TypeScript interfaces.

title: Navigation structure — Tab + Stack navigators
description: Set up React Navigation with a bottom tab navigator (Home, Transactions, Import, Settings) and nested stack navigators for each tab. Wire up placeholder screens. Configure theme via React Native Paper Provider.

title: SQLite migration runner and v1 schema
description: Create db/database.ts singleton, db/migrations/migrate.ts runner that applies versioned migrations in order, and db/migrations/v1_initial.ts with the full schema from ARCHITECTURE.md (categories, people, transactions, transaction_people, events, allocations, savings_goals) plus seeded default categories.

title: Initialize Expo project with dependencies
description: Run expo init, install expo-sqlite, zustand, react-navigation/native, react-navigation/bottom-tabs, react-navigation/stack, react-native-paper, date-fns, expo-document-picker, expo-crypto. Configure app.json (name, slug, icon placeholder).

title: EPIC: Iteration 1 — Core Transactions (Income & Expense CRUD)
description: Full add/edit/delete for income and expense transactions. Includes date picker, category picker, people tagging, monthly list view, and monthly summary. This is the deliverable for Iteration 1.

title: EPIC: Project Foundation — Expo + SQLite + Navigation setup
description: Set up the bare Expo React Native project with all dependencies, SQLite migration runner, Zustand stores, React Navigation tab structure, and React Native Paper theme. This is the foundation all other features depend on.

title: PDF bank statement parser and import review UI
description: services/pdfParser.ts: regex-based parser that splits extracted text into transaction rows (date, description, debit/credit amount). Supports common Indian bank statement formats (HDFC, SBI, ICICI, Axis — column-based and narrative formats). ImportReviewScreen: shows parsed rows as a checklist, auto-suggests category from description keywords, user can correct category and type, then confirm to bulk-insert into SQLite.

title: PDF import — file picker and PDF.js text extraction
description: PdfImportScreen: button to open expo-document-picker (PDF only). Selected file loaded into a hidden WebView running PDF.js (bundled asset). JS bridge (window.ReactNativeWebView.postMessage) sends back extracted plain text page by page. Handle errors: password-protected PDFs, corrupt files.

title: Home screen — monthly summary and quick-add FAB
description: HomeScreen shows: current month name, total income, total expense, net balance (SummaryBar component). Below: last 5 transactions. FAB (+) opens AddEditTransactionScreen. Month navigator to browse historical months.

title: Transaction list screen with month navigation and filters
description: TransactionListScreen shows all transactions for the selected month. Month navigator (← Month Year →) at top. Filter chips: All / Income / Expense. Each row is a TransactionCard showing date, description, category icon+color, amount (green for income, red for expense). Tap → edit, long-press → delete confirmation.

title: AddEditTransaction screen
description: Full-screen modal for creating and editing a transaction. Fields: type toggle (Income/Expense), amount (numeric keyboard), date (DateTimePicker), category (bottom sheet with category list), description (optional text input), people tags (multi-select chips). Validates amount > 0 and category required. On save calls transactionStore.add or .update.

title: EPIC: Iteration 1b — PDF Bank Statement Import
description: Allow user to pick a PDF bank statement, extract transaction rows using PDF.js in a WebView, show a review screen with auto-suggested categories, then bulk-insert confirmed transactions into SQLite.

title: People management screen
description: Settings > People: list all people. Add new person (name, optional phone/email). Edit and delete. People are shown as chips in the AddEditTransaction screen for tagging.

title: Categories management screen
description: Settings > Categories: list all categories grouped by type (income/expense). Add new category (name, type, color picker, icon picker). Edit name/color/icon. Cannot delete system categories (is_system=1). Custom categories can be deleted if no transactions reference them (or offer reassign).
